model.name: lfm — architecture
model.documentation: Blazor WebAssembly SPA, Azure Functions isolated-worker backend, Cosmos DB + Blob Storage data stores, deployed to Azure via GitHub Actions. Single-plateau (Production). Hobby-tier cost envelope.

element.name: Lfm API
element.documentation: Azure Functions isolated-worker, .NET 10. api/Lfm.Api.csproj. 31 HTTP endpoints + 2 timer triggers.

element.name: Lfm App (Blazor WASM)
element.documentation: Blazor WebAssembly standalone SPA, .NET 10. app/Lfm.App.csproj. Fluent UI components, JSON-backed i18n (en, fi), session-aware auth.

element.name: Lfm App Core (library)
element.documentation: Framework-neutral services library. app/Lfm.App.Core/Lfm.App.Core.csproj. Typed HttpClients, auth state provider, i18n, data cache. Mutation-tested separately.

element.name: Lfm Contracts (library)
element.documentation: Shared DTOs, enums, FluentValidation rules. shared/Lfm.Contracts/Lfm.Contracts.csproj. No upstream dependencies.

element.name: Battle.net Authentication
element.documentation: OAuth sign-in, callback, logout, session management.

element.name: Battle.net Character Sync
element.documentation: List, refresh, and fetch portrait URLs for the user's WoW characters.

element.name: Raider Profile
element.documentation: Current user profile read, update, delete.

element.name: Guild
element.documentation: Guild home (read, update settings) and guild admin roster.

element.name: Raider Characters
element.documentation: Add, read, update, enrich characters on a raider record.

element.name: Runs
element.documentation: List, read, create, update, delete, sign up, cancel signup for raid runs.

element.name: WoW Reference Data
element.documentation: Serve cached instances, expansions, specializations; admin refresh; weekly timer refresh.

element.name: Admin
element.documentation: Schema migration for runs; reference-data refresh triggers.

element.name: Health
element.documentation: Liveness (static) and readiness (Cosmos probe).

element.name: Privacy Contact
element.documentation: Return the privacy contact email for legal inquiries.

element.name: GET /api/e2e/login
element.documentation: Test-only endpoint; routed only when LFM__E2E=true.

element.name: Log Analytics workspace
element.documentation: PerGB2018, 1 GB/day ingestion cap, 30-day retention. Receives diagnostic settings from all downstream resources. infra/modules/loganalytics.bicep.

element.name: Application Insights (workspace-linked)
element.documentation: Workspace-based ingestion shares the 5 GB/month free tier with Log Analytics. DisableLocalAuth=true — metrics publish via managed identity. infra/modules/functions.bicep (components sub-resource).

element.name: Key Vault
element.documentation: Standard SKU, RBAC-authorised, 90-day soft-delete, purge protection, CanNotDelete lock. infra/modules/keyvault.bicep.

element.name: Cosmos DB account (NoSQL)
element.documentation: enableFreeTier=true (1,000 RU/s + 25 GB). disableLocalAuth=true — RBAC-only. Session consistency. Network ACL 0.0.0.0 + AzureServices bypass. CanNotDelete lock. infra/modules/cosmos.bicep.

element.name: Storage account
element.documentation: StorageV2, Standard_LRS, allowSharedKeyAccess=false — RBAC-only via managed identity. TLS 1.2, HTTPS-only, public blob access disabled. 7-day blob/container soft delete. CanNotDelete lock. infra/modules/storage.bicep.

element.name: App Service Plan (Y1 Consumption)
element.documentation: Dynamic SKU Y1 — Linux Consumption. 1M free executions/month + free grants. infra/modules/functions.bicep serverFarm.

element.name: Function App
element.documentation: kind=functionapp,linux. HTTPS-only. FTP/SCM disabled. HTTP/2, TLS 1.2 min. SystemAssigned managed identity. Health check /api/health. infra/modules/functions.bicep site.

element.name: Static Web App (Free)
element.documentation: Free tier. Hosts Blazor WASM published output. TLS and global CDN included. infra/modules/swa.bicep.

element.name: Azure Functions runtime — dotnet-isolated v4
element.documentation: FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION=~4, FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME=dotnet-isolated, WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE=1.

element.name: dataprotection key (RSA-2048, wrap/unwrap)
element.documentation: ASP.NET Core Data Protection master key. wrapKey/unwrapKey only.

element.name: Cosmos SQL database
element.documentation: Database name is set via Cosmos__DatabaseName; defaults differ between production and E2E.

element.name: raiders container
element.documentation: Partition key /battleNetId. Per-document TTL. Indexes the lastSeenAt path only.

element.name: runs container
element.documentation: Partition key /id. Per-document TTL. Composite index on visibility + creatorGuild + startTime.

element.name: guilds container
element.documentation: Partition key /id. Point-read only; no secondary indexes.

element.name: wow blob container
element.documentation: WoW reference data (journal-instance, playable-specialization, playable-class, playable-race, hero-talent-tree) cached under wow/reference/{kind}/.

element.name: dataprotection blob container
element.documentation: ASP.NET Core Data Protection key ring XML, wrapped by the Key Vault key.

element.name: deployments blob container
element.documentation: Reserved for future deployment artefacts.

element.name: Deploy (orchestrator)
element.documentation: .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Change-detecting orchestrator. Concurrency group=deploy, cancel-in-progress=false — serialises deploys.

element.name: Deploy Infrastructure
element.documentation: .github/workflows/deploy-infra.yml. Bicep what-if preview + apply to the target resource group.

element.name: Deploy App — Build
element.documentation: .github/workflows/deploy-app-build.yml. dotnet publish API + App, unit tests, bundle-size gate. Produces two artifacts.

element.name: Deploy App
element.documentation: .github/workflows/deploy-app.yml. Deploys API to Azure Functions, App to Azure Static Web Apps. Health-checks the API.

element.name: deploy-app-publish-api (Functions zip)
element.documentation: Uploaded by deploy-app-build.yml, consumed by deploy-app.yml via download-artifact. 1-day retention.

element.name: deploy-app-publish-app (SWA publish dir)
element.documentation: Uploaded by deploy-app-build.yml, consumed by deploy-app.yml via download-artifact. 1-day retention.

element.name: Production
element.documentation: The only Plateau in this pipeline. No environment: fields used in any workflow; Azure auth is OIDC to a single resource group. No dev/staging tier and no Gap between Plateaus.

element.name: Raid Logistics
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder. Architect: name the business service(s) this platform realises and link to the Lfm API Application Services via Realisation in a dedicated Application-to-Business Realisation view.

element.name: Guild Roster Management
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder inferred from the Guild Application Service.

element.name: Character Profile Sync
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder inferred from the Battle.net Character Sync Application Service.

element.name: Identity and Access
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder inferred from the Battle.net Authentication Application Service.

element.name: Raider
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder for the end user of the platform — a WoW raider who signs in with Battle.net and manages runs and characters.

element.name: Guild Administrator
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder for the Raider who also administers a guild (/guild/admin, /admin/reference).

element.name: Site Administrator
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder for the SiteAdminService-authorised operator (WoW reference refresh, schema migration).

element.name: Hobby-tier cost envelope
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: inferred from CLAUDE.md. "Hobby project. Prefer free tiers." Drives the Cosmos free-tier, Functions Consumption, SWA Free, and Log Analytics 5 GB/month decisions visible in the Technology Layer. Architect: refine into Goal/Requirement/Constraint as appropriate.

element.name: All data-plane access via managed identity
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: inferred from Bicep (disableLocalAuth on Cosmos, allowSharedKeyAccess=false on Storage, RBAC-only on Key Vault, DisableLocalAuth on Application Insights). Architect: confirm as an intentional architectural norm or rewrite.

element.name: Raid Planning
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder Capability for the Capability Map view. Architect fills in.

element.name: Roster Management
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder Capability.

element.name: Character Insight
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder Capability.

element.name: Identity and Session
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY: placeholder Capability.

view.name: lfm — Application Cooperation
view.documentation: The four Application Components and the ten API Services they cooperate through. Deliberate omission from this view (still in the model, still drift-checked): the 33 API Interfaces and their Composition / Serving edges — placing 33 interface boxes plus the fan of edges onto them made every other arrow unreadable. Interfaces live in the model's `<elements>` block; architects consult them in Archi's tree view or via grep. Application Cooperation should answer "how do the components cooperate", not "what is every API route".

view.name: lfm — Technology Realisation
view.documentation: What hosts what, what composes what, and what the API consumes at runtime. App is hosted above SWA; API is hosted above Function App (Assignments are short verticals). Data-store Nodes span a wide row below the host band; Artifacts sit in horizontal rows under their parent Nodes (not stacks) so Composition lines don't cross sibling Artifacts. The four API→DataStore Serving edges are routed through three element-free gaps: between SWA and App Service Plan on the west, the Function App / Runtime split in the middle, and east of Runtime. Bendpoints force each line up from the API, along the open band between host and data rows, and down into its target.

view.name: lfm — Migration
view.documentation: Single-Plateau Migration View. No dev/staging/prod tier exists — Azure auth is OIDC to one resource group. Layout reads left-to-right in time order: orchestrator → sub-Work-Packages → Deliverables → Implementation Events → Plateau. Deliverables sit between WP-build and WP-app so the Flow arrow runs left-to-right (not doubling back).

view.name: lfm — Capability Map (FORWARD-ONLY)
view.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY scaffold. Capabilities inferred from Application Service domain names; Business Services inferred from Application Component names. Architect fills in with real strategy vocabulary; the skill cannot extract Strategy or Business elements from source code.
